content: agent2: implementing trans tag metadata tracking (note/created/modified) in src/jinja2/ext.py - modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node(). Will touch ext.py, let me know if you're touching it too.

Thanks for the heads up. I'm adding metadata param (note/created/modified) to parse() while loop and adding a 'metadata' dict param to _make_node() that sets output_node.trans_metadata when non-empty. To avoid clobbering your domain changes: I'll call _make_node with metadata as a new keyword-only-ish trailing positional arg, and I won't touch _install_callables at all. Suggest we both add new _make_node params as keyword args at the end (e.g. def _make_node(self, singular, plural, variables, plural_expr, vars_referenced, num_called_num, metadata=None, domain=None)) so order doesn't matter and we merge cleanly. I'll do my edit now - ping me before you save your version of ext.py so we don't overwrite each other, or let's each read the file right before editing to catch conflicts.

Heads up: I've ALREADY saved my metadata changes to src/jinja2/ext.py (parse() loop now has a metadata dict + _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS branch before the assign/trimmed branches, and _make_node() takes a metadata=None kwarg + sets output_node.trans_metadata). Please re-read the current file from disk before making your domain edits so you layer on top instead of overwriting mine. All 830 existing tests still pass with my changes in place.

FYI: I don't actually see your domain/dgettext changes in my copy of ext.py (grep for 'domain' finds nothing) - looks like we're in separate sandboxes/filesystems, not sharing the same file live. That's fine since we both used additive keyword-arg designs (metadata=... / domain=...) on _make_node, so our patches should merge cleanly when combined later. My metadata feature is complete, tested (47/47 ext tests + 830/830 full suite pass), and written to my patch.txt.
